scenario: Helios Bank ships a Camunda 8.8 DMN decision that derives a tier label from an applicant profile for 4500 claims per day. The architect adds a literal expression variable to a downstream decision and wants to follow the DMN naming guidance so dependent decisions can keep referencing the result without rewriting their FEEL expressions later on.
question: Which guidance does the DMN docs give for choosing the variable name of a decision literal expression?

options.text: Use the FEEL function name from the literal expression as the variable name so the engine can match outputs to expressions
Use a kebab-case form of the decision name so dependent decisions can reference it via dotted syntax across the DRG model
Use a numeric prefix on the variable name so result lookups remain stable when the decision id is later renamed by another team
Use the decision id as the variable name so dependent decisions can reference the result by that shared identifier later

a.text: Incorrect. The docs do not tie the variable name to a FEEL function name; the recommended practice is to reuse the decision id as the variable name of the literal expression.

b.text: Incorrect. The kebab-case format is explicitly disallowed for the variable name because the dash is the FEEL subtraction operator; whitespace and other symbols are also forbidden.

c.text: Incorrect. The docs say nothing about numeric prefixes; the recommendation is to use the decision id as the variable name so dependent decisions can reference the result.

d.text: Correct. The docs recommend using the decision id as the variable name, since dependent decisions reference the literal expression result by the variable name rather than by the decision id.

explanation: The DMN docs recommend reusing the decision id as the variable name of a literal expression because dependent decisions reference the result by the variable name, not by the decision id; this keeps downstream FEEL expressions stable.
